prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9948) and high seasonality variance (0.9888), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, CWT_rf, CWT_svr), suggesting per-horizon selection would help. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, up, down). Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "ARIMA", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "NaiveSeasonal"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9948,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9948) and high seasonality variance (0.9888), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, CWT_rf, CWT_svr), suggesting per-horizon selection would help. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, up, down). Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong per-horizon heterogeneity, favoring best_per_horizon and topk_mean approaches. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination due to high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9948) and high seasonality variance (0.9888). The RMSE spread between ETS (8222.52) and FT_catboost (17706.64) is large (~115%), justifying rmse_focus preset and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.35 to balance extremes. ridge_stacking adds diversity via meta-learning. Early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, CWT_rf), making topk_mean_k3 appropriate for robust selection."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5) = 4 different types. No redundancy issues. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names. n_windows=3, using expanding rolling with train_window=5 - selection/weighted strategies use past windows only (no leakage). Current best is ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 (score 0.8304) which beats universe top candidates like exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants (score ~0.811-0.813). No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9948) and high seasonality variance (0.9888), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, CWT_rf, CWT_svr), suggesting per-horizon selection would help. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, up, down). Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong per-horizon heterogeneity, favoring best_per_horizon and topk_mean approaches. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination due to high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9948) and high seasonality variance (0.9888). The RMSE spread between ETS (8222.52) and FT_catboost (17706.64) is large (~115%), justifying rmse_focus preset and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.35 to balance extremes. ridge_stacking adds diversity via meta-learning. Early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, CWT_rf), making topk_mean_k3 appropriate for robust selection.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5) = 4 different types. No redundancy issues. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names. n_windows=3, using expanding rolling with train_window=5 - selection/weighted strategies use past windows only (no leakage). Current best is ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 (score 0.8304) which beats universe top candidates like exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants (score ~0.811-0.813). No changes needed.
